REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT AND NICHOLAS ANDRADE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, AS ALLEGED, ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (SEE PARAGRAPH 25 AND 32 "DEFENDANT CAUSED PLAINTIFF TO QUIT THE SUBJECT PROPERTY INVOLUNTARILY AND IN VIOLATION OF THE RENT ORDINANCE'. BY THEIR FAILURE TO ACT APPROPRIATELY TO REPAIR THE SUBJECT PROPERTY."). PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10-DAY LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS RE: 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, E.G. EVICTION UNDER THE PRETEXT OF REMOVING THE UNIT FROM HOUSING USE; RETALIATION; AND DELAYED DISCOVERY OF FACTS GIVING RISE TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT AND NICHOLAS ANDRADE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY HAS SHIFTED ITS BURDEN. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. SFRO 37.9(A)(10) REQUIRES THAT THE "RENTAL UNIT" (DEFINED IN SFRO 37.2) BE REMOVED FROM "HOUSING USE." PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT "HOUSING USE" OF THE "SPACE" CONSTITUTES A VIOLATION IS NOT SUPPORTED BY THE LANGUAGE OF SFRO 37.9(A)(10) OR ANY OTHER AUTHORITY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: CAROL NARABURDA CSR#8052 (925)360-6065. (501)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Aug-16-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Steven Egler, (510)550-8557 for Defendants/Moving parties James McDermott and Nichlas Andrade.
Robert Smith, (415)294-1465 for Plaintiff/Opposing pary Ana-Maria Guerrero.

Argured by Robert Smith counsel for plaintiff. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Moving party has shifted its burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue of fact. SFRO 37.9(a)(10) requires that the "rental unit" (defined in SFRO 37.2) be removed from "housing use." Plaintiff's argument that "housing use" of the "space" constitutes a violation is not supported by the language of SFRO 37.9(a)(10) or any other authority. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Carol Naraburda CSR#8052 (925)360-6065. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Set For Trial On Apr-18-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Wiederhold, Esq.(415-533-0735) and Robert Smith, Esq. (415-294-1465) for Plaintiff/opposing party Ana Maria Guerrero.
Steven Abern, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP (510-763-2324) for Defendant/moving party JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE.

The Court reads the tentative ruling. Defendant argues the statute of limitations. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges discovery of the facts giving rise to causes of 3 and 4 no earlier than June 2014. Arguments brought up for the first time in reply are not considered. Unauthorized sur-opposition and sur-reply are stricken. Order is signed in open court.(see scanned order for details). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Wiederhold, Esq.(415-533-0735) and Robert Smith, Esq. (415-294-1465) for Plaintiff/opposing party Ana Maria Guerrero.
Steven Abern, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP (510-763-2324) for Defendant/moving party JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE.

The Court reads the tentative ruling. Defendant argues the statute of limitations. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE's Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is DENIED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges discovery of the facts giving rise to causes of 3 and 4 no earlier than June 2014. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Continued To Jun-15-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances by either party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS JAMES MCDERMOTT AND NICHOLAS ANDRADE'S Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is continued to June 15, 2015 on Court's own motion. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk:N. Wong, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Sheriff Abern, Esq. of Haaapala Thompson & Abern LLP, (510-763-2324), for James McDermott and Nicholas Andrade, Defendants and moving parties.
Robert Smith, Esq. of Law Offices of Mary Catherine Wiederhold, (415-294-1465), for Ana Maria Guerrero, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Plaintiff submits. Defendant argues that demurrer should be sustained without leave to amend.
Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff's 3rd and 4th causes of action, as alleged, are barred by the statute of limitations. (See paragraph 25 and 32 "Defendant caused Plaintiff to quit the subject property involuntarily and in violation of the Rent Ordinance'. by their failure to act appropriately to repair the subject property."). Plaintiff shall have 10-day leave to amend to allege in good faith facts re: 3rd and 4th causes of action, e.g. eviction under the pretext of removing the unit from housing use; retaliation; and delayed discovery of facts giving rise to these causes of action. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
